families.example:
flowchart TD
  A[Start] --> B{Ship?}
  B -->|yes| C[Deploy]
  B -->|no| D[Fix]

stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> Draft
  Draft --> Review : submit
  Review --> [*] : approve

sequenceDiagram
  participant U as User
  participant S as Server
  U->>S: request
  S-->>U: response

timeline
  title Roadmap
  2025 : Alpha : Beta
  2026 : GA

journey
  title Checkout
  section Browse
    Find product: 4: Shopper
  section Buy
    Pay: 3: Shopper

architecture-beta
  group api(cloud)[API]
  service db(database)[Database] in api
  service disk(disk)[Storage] in api
  db:L -- R:disk

xychart-beta
  title "Revenue"
  x-axis [Q1, Q2, Q3]
  y-axis "USD" 0 --> 100
  bar [45, 62, 80]

quadrantChart
  title Prioritize
  x-axis Low Effort --> High Effort
  y-axis Low Value --> High Value
  Quick win: [0.2, 0.8]
  Money pit: [0.8, 0.2]

gantt
  title Plan
  dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
  section Build
  Implement :a1, 2026-01-05, 5d
  Review :after a1, 2d

warningCodes.what: the source parses to a diagram with no drawable content.
warningCodes.triggers: A bare header like `flowchart TD` with no statements after it, a body containing only comments, or a mutation sequence that removed the last node, message, or task.
warningCodes.fix: Add at least one element — `add_node`/`add_edge` for flowcharts, `add_participant`/`add_message` for sequence, `add_task` for gantt/journey — or check that the intended body was not lost before serializing.

warningCodes.what: a gantt schedule cannot be resolved to concrete dates, so bars cannot be positioned.
warningCodes.triggers: A task whose `after`/`until` expression references a task id that does not exist, or a start/end that cannot be parsed against the declared `dateFormat`. The warning carries the engine reason string.
warningCodes.fix: Point the reference at a real task id or give the task explicit dates with the `set_task_dates` mutation (or edit the offending source line named in the reason).
warningCodes.example:
gantt
  title Release
  dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
  section Build
    Ship :ship, after review, 3d

warningCodes.what: the diagram parsed, but rendering to the requested format threw before producing an artifact.
warningCodes.triggers: A construct that parses but the renderer cannot lay out or rasterize — an unsupported combination reaching the SVG/PNG path, or a size/raster budget hit on a very large diagram. Not reachable from small well-formed source in normal operation.
warningCodes.fix: Return the structured error and the source rather than a fabricated artifact; simplify or split the diagram, drop the construct named in the message, or fall back to a lighter format (SVG or ASCII before PNG). A reproducible failure on stable source is a renderer bug worth reporting.

warningCodes.what: an edge, message, or dependency references an endpoint that is not in the diagram.
warningCodes.triggers: A gantt `after` dependency naming a missing task, a sequence message whose participant was removed, or edges left dangling after a `remove_node` mutation.
warningCodes.fix: Add the missing endpoint (`add_node`, `add_participant`, `add_task`) or retarget/remove the dangling edge (`remove_edge`, `remove_message`).
warningCodes.example:
gantt
  dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
  section Build
    Design :design, 2026-01-01, 2d
    Ship :ship, after review, 3d

warningCodes.what: a positioned node extends past the computed canvas on the reported axis.
warningCodes.triggers: Never in normal operation — the engine sizes the canvas around content, so this is a tripwire that fires only when a layout pass moves geometry after the canvas was sized. Layout is deterministic, so a firing input reproduces byte-identically.
warningCodes.fix: Not fixable by editing the diagram content itself: simplify or remove the construct that provokes it, and report the source as a renderer bug so the layout defect gets fixed.

warningCodes.what: a node that belongs to a subgraph or group is positioned outside its group rectangle.
warningCodes.triggers: An engine-bug tripwire like `OFF_CANVAS`: deeply nested subgraphs combined with cross-group edges are historically where containment slipped. The warning names both the group and the escaping member.
warningCodes.fix: Flatten the nesting or move the member out of the group (source edit, or `remove_node` then re-add outside the subgraph). A reproducible breach is a renderer bug worth reporting with the source.

warningCodes.what: a node carries a shape outside the renderer’s known vocabulary and falls back to a plain rectangle.
warningCodes.triggers: Shape syntax the parser modeled but the renderer does not draw — typically newer Mermaid shape names reaching a structured flowchart or state graph.
warningCodes.fix: Switch the node to a supported shape (rectangle, rounded, diamond, stadium, circle, hexagon, cylinder, …) with a source edit; the diagram still renders meanwhile, so this is a warning rather than an error.

warningCodes.what:
a label’s longest rendered line exceeds the character cap (default 40), which hurts layout and readability. The count measures what the renderer draws: `
` starts a new line, XML entities like ` ` count as one character, and formatting tags are stripped.
warningCodes.triggers: Prose sentences pasted into node labels, edge labels, message text, or section/period titles — common when an agent copies requirement text verbatim into the diagram. Multi-line labels fire only when a single rendered line exceeds the cap.
warningCodes.fix: Shorten the text with `set_label`, `set_message_text`, or the matching family mutation; raise `labelCharCap` in `VerifyOptions` only when long labels are genuinely intended.
warningCodes.example:
flowchart LR
  A[This label is far longer than the forty character cap] --> B[Done]

warningCodes.what: two nodes’ boxes intersect in the final layout; the warning reports the pair and the overlap area in pixels.
warningCodes.triggers: The deterministic layout separates nodes by construction, so no small flowchart source fires this — it appears only when a family adapter or post-pass produces colliding boxes on dense inputs. It is a tripwire, not an everyday lint.
warningCodes.fix: Shorten the labels of the named pair or reduce local density; if the overlap persists on a stable input, treat it as a layout defect and report the source.

warningCodes.what: a single edge’s routed polyline crosses over itself.
warningCodes.triggers: A routing tripwire on the final geometry: the router avoids self-intersection, so a firing means dense cyclic routing degraded. The warning names the edge and the crossing count.
warningCodes.fix: Remove or redirect the redundant edge (`remove_edge`, then `add_edge` along a simpler path); a persistent self-cross on unchanged source is an engine bug to report.

warningCodes.what: an edge deviates from its certified straight lane by more than the tolerance, reported in pixels.
warningCodes.triggers: The layout certifies clear lanes when routes are frozen; a hitch means a later pass mutated geometry after certification. Agents cannot cause this from source alone.
warningCodes.fix: Simplify the crossing edges near the named edge if a quick fix is needed, and report the reproducing source — the certificate/geometry mismatch is an engine defect.

warningCodes.what: an orthogonally-routed edge contains a bend its route certificate does not explain.
warningCodes.triggers: Orthogonal families (class, ER) certify every bend against an obstacle; an unexplained bend means post-certification geometry drift. Not reachable from well-formed source in normal operation.
warningCodes.fix: No source-level fix is expected to be needed; if verify reports it, capture the source and report it as a renderer bug — determinism makes the reproduction exact.

warningCodes.what: an edge label sits on a line segment shared with another edge, so it is ambiguous which edge it names.
warningCodes.triggers: Fan-in/fan-out patterns where several labeled edges merge onto a shared trunk and a label pill lands on the shared piece rather than the edge’s own segment.
warningCodes.fix: Shorten the label with `set_label` so it fits the edge’s exclusive segment, or restructure the fan so the labeled edge has its own approach.

warningCodes.what: an edge attached to a subgraph or group does not terminate on the container’s border.
warningCodes.triggers: Container-anchored edges must end exactly on the group rectangle; a miss means the border moved after routing. This is a tripwire over final geometry rather than a source mistake.
warningCodes.fix: Re-anchor the edge to a member node instead of the container as a workaround, and report the source — the container anchor contract is the engine’s to uphold.

warningCodes.what: an edge endpoint does not sit on the outline of the node shape it connects to (e.g. off a diamond’s facet).
warningCodes.triggers: Endpoint-on-shape is checked against the final node geometry; a miss usually accompanies a node that changed size or shape after routes were frozen.
warningCodes.fix: Switching the node to a simpler shape (rectangle) is the mechanical workaround; the underlying anchor drift is an engine defect worth reporting with the source.

warningCodes.what: an edge still follows a corridor computed before its node moved, so it anchors where the node used to be.
warningCodes.triggers: A compaction or alignment pass moved a node after edge routing without re-anchoring the affected edges. Not producible from source alone in normal operation.
warningCodes.fix: No diagram edit reliably clears it; report the reproducing source. If it blocks a task, removing and re-adding the named edge forces a fresh route.

warningCodes.what: two edges with identical endpoints, label, and style — the second adds ink but no information.
warningCodes.triggers: An agent re-adding an edge that already exists, typically an `add_edge` issued without checking the current edge list, or copy-pasted source lines.
warningCodes.fix: Remove one copy with `remove_edge` (or delete the duplicate source line); if two parallel edges are intentional, give them distinct labels so they stop being duplicates.
warningCodes.example:
flowchart LR
  A[Start] --> B[Finish]
  A --> B

warningCodes.what: a node cannot be reached from any root (a node with no incoming edges) by following edges.
warningCodes.triggers: Disconnected clusters left behind after `remove_edge`/`remove_node` mutations, or a cycle with no entry edge from the main flow.
warningCodes.fix: Connect the node into the flow with `add_edge` from a reachable node, or delete it with `remove_node` if it is leftover.
warningCodes.example:
flowchart LR
  A[Start] --> B[Done]
  C[Orphan] --> D[Cycle]
  D --> C

warningCodes.what: a decision diamond with two or more exits has at least one unlabeled exit, so the branch condition is ambiguous.
warningCodes.triggers: Adding a second exit to a diamond without a condition label. ISO 5807 / ANSI X3.5 require each exit of a multi-exit decision to be labeled with its condition value.
warningCodes.fix: Label every exit — `set_label` on the unlabeled edge (e.g. `yes`/`no`) or add `|condition|` to the source line.
warningCodes.example:
flowchart TD
  A{Ready?} -->|yes| B[Ship]
  A --> C[Wait]

warningCodes.what: in-body %% comments will not survive structured serialization; the loss is announced, not silent.
warningCodes.triggers: Comments between statements in a structurally-modeled body: the typed tree does not model them, so `serializeMermaid` writes the body back without them. The warning carries the count and line numbers.
warningCodes.fix: Move essential comment content into a label or title before mutating, keep a source-level edit instead of a typed mutation when comments must survive, or accept the loss knowingly.
warningCodes.example:
flowchart LR
  %% review note that structured serialization drops
  A[Start] --> B[Done]

warningCodes.what: the source uses syntax or content the local structured model cannot faithfully express.
warningCodes.triggers: Flowchart `click`/`href` directives, edge IDs and edge metadata, v11 `@{ shape: … }` node metadata, markdown strings, unclosed delimiters that would silently drop content, or `syntax: "empty_layout"` when content-bearing source lays out to a 0×0 canvas with no nodes, edges, or groups.
warningCodes.fix: For preserved Mermaid syntax, remove the directive if local rendering fidelity matters, or keep it knowing the local renderer ignores it; edits touching those lines need source-level editing rather than typed mutations. For `empty_layout`, inspect the warning message and `verify.layout`, then repair the malformed or unsupported source before accepting the artifact.
warningCodes.example:
flowchart LR
  A[Start] --> B[Docs]
  click B "https://example.com"

warningCodes.what: a parse → serialize → re-parse cycle lost nodes, edges, or groups by count.
warningCodes.triggers: A serializer defect on unusual syntax: the faithfulness tally before and after the round trip disagrees. This is a tripwire that guards every verify; it should not fire on supported syntax.
warningCodes.fix: Do not serialize the typed tree for this diagram — fall back to source-level edits so nothing is lost, and report the source; the before/after counts on the warning pinpoint what vanished.
